title: gitmap startup-list --json output
description: Array of gitmap-managed autostart entries discovered on the current host. On Linux: parsed from XDG .desktop files in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/autostart that carry the X-Gitmap-Managed=true marker. On macOS: parsed from ~/Library/LaunchAgents .plist files containing the Gitmap-Managed key. On Windows: enumerated from BOTH the HKCU Run-key (with sibling .gitmap-managed marker + tracking subkey under HKCU\Software\Gitmap\StartupRegistry) AND the Startup folder .lnk shortcuts (tracked under HKCU\Software\Gitmap\StartupFolder). Empty result is encoded as `[]` (NOT `null`) so jq pipelines can `length` without conditionals. Backed by gitmap/stablejson — key order is contractual and pinned by gitmap/cmd/startuplist_jsonschema_contract_test.go.

items.title: Startup entry
items.description: A single gitmap-managed autostart entry. The same shape is emitted on every OS so cross-platform consumers can parse one schema.

name.description: Stable entry identifier. On Linux/macOS this is the filename without the .desktop / .plist extension. On Windows registry backend this is the Run-key value name (always prefixed with `gitmap-`). On Windows startup-folder backend this is the .lnk filename without the .lnk extension. Safe to use as a primary key for downstream deduplication.

path.description: Filesystem-or-registry locator the user can inspect manually. Linux/macOS: absolute filesystem path to the .desktop / .plist file. Windows registry: full registry value path of the form `HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\gitmap-<name>`. Windows startup-folder: absolute filesystem path to the .lnk shortcut. Always non-empty.

exec.description: The command line that will run at user login. Linux/macOS: the Exec= / ProgramArguments value. Windows registry backend: the string value stored under the Run-key. Windows startup-folder backend: ALWAYS the empty string — recovering the original target from a .lnk would require parsing the [MS-SHLLINK] binary format which gitmap deliberately avoids; consumers needing the target should read it from the tracking subkey under HKCU\Software\Gitmap\StartupFolder\<name>\Exec instead. May be empty.
